mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals a structural cooling of the labor market that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the threshold crossing, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion across durable and non-durable sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or housing starts and retail sales diverge without impacting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average signals resilient manufacturing capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when FEDFUNDS is rising, or 5-day forward returns are flat/negative despite industrial production outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.0% reflects sustained aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth above 2.0% (Spearman rho < 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 205.0 triggers market expectations of prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which increases equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (K-S test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates aggressive labor market tightening that boosts household income and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary regime, flat retail sales alongside rising inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or equity returns remain positive despite margin compression indicators.
